seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: OpenAI Assistants API ai coding assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful code generation, repository-aware debugging, and sandboxed execution. However, risks like unsafe code execution, credential leakage, and hallucinated function calls can lead to security breaches or incorrect outputs. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on OpenAI's Assistants API, which manages threads, runs, and tools like code interpreter and file search. Users upload repository files, initiate threads with coding tasks, and the assistant iteratively generates, tests, and debugs code in a sandbox. Function calling integrates external tools for linting or testing, with run completion triggering verification.

Key components include persistent threads for conversation history, sandboxed code execution for safe testing, and file search for repository context. The agent loop handles tool calls, code validation, and iterative refinement until tests pass.

Vex integrates as a webhook on run completion, verifying generated code against security, correctness, and compliance checks before returning results to the user.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate code that attempts file system access, network calls, or exec() despite sandbox limits
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code AST for dangerous operations and simulates execution in isolated environment

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Vector search returns deprecated APIs or wrong repo files, leading to incorrect code suggestions
risks.mitigation: Vex validates file relevance via semantic similarity and recency checks against git history

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long debugging sessions accumulate messages exceeding token limits without truncation warnings
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers summarization or pruning before run completion

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Assistant generates invalid JSON parameters for testing/linting tools, causing runtime failures
risks.mitigation: Vex validates function call JSON against OpenAPI schemas before execution

implementation.title: Create Thread and Upload Repository Files
implementation.description: Start a conversation thread and attach repository files for context-aware coding
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

# Upload repo files
file_streams = [
    open(f"repo/{f}", "rb") for f in repo_files
]
file_ids = [
    client.files.create(f, purpose="assistants").id
    for f in file_streams
]

client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role="user",
    content="Fix this bug in main.py and add tests:",
    file_ids=file_ids
)

vexIntegration.title: Configure Vex Webhook on Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification as a webhook endpoint for all assistant run completions
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient

from flask import Flask, request
app = Flask(__name__)
vex = VexClient(api_key="vex-api-key")

@app.route('/vex-webhook', methods=['POST'])
def vex_webhook():
    event = request.json
    if event['type'] == 'run.completed':
        run = event['data']
        messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(run['thread_id'])
        
        # Extract generated code
        code_content = extract_code_from_messages(messages)
        
        # Verify with Vex
        result = vex.verify_code(
            code=code_content,
            specs={
                'correctness': 'high',
                'security': 'critical',
                'no_secrets': True,
                'license_compliance': 'mit'
            }
        )
        
        if not result.passed:
            # Reject unsafe code
            client.beta.threads.runs.cancel(run['thread_id'], run['id'])
    return 'OK', 200
